scenarios.description: Shared auth-status and resolver-backed launch dry-runs report provider and shared credential posture across adapters
macOS secretless smoke launches Workcell-managed Codex, Claude, and Gemini entrypoints against the real runtime and version probes each provider
macOS secretless smoke prepares and launches the explicit Docker Desktop compat target against the real runtime and version probes each provider
Host-side auth commands manage explicit policy-backed credential inputs without launching the runtime
Host-side policy commands render, validate, diff, and explain merged credential policy without launching the runtime
Claude resolver-backed auth fails closed without an export and resolves through the launcher staging path when the test export hook is present
Codex host-auth resolver reports launch readiness from the host and resolves through the launcher staging path with a reviewed host cache file
Host-side publish-pr covers dry-run command rendering, main-only base enforcement, and the explicit lower-assurance non-main draft handoff with repo hooks bypassed
Host-side publish-github-release only mutates draft releases and fails closed for already-published mutable or immutable releases
Shared home-control-plane manifest parsing uses one-pass jq readers for copy and ssh entries
Dry-run scenarios surface prompt-autonomy, development, breakglass, readonly, arbitrary-command, and control-plane VCS assurance labels across adapters
Docker Desktop compatibility target selection keeps target metadata and state roots explicit, and fails closed when the reviewed host matrix blocks the selected target
AWS remote VM preview target selection keeps the brokered target metadata, support boundary, and certification-only live gate explicit while allowing deterministic dry-run planning
AWS EC2 SSM certification smoke proves a reviewed target is SSM-online, has no inbound security-group rules, uses AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore, and matches the Workcell preview broker plan
GCP remote VM preview target selection keeps brokered IAP target metadata, support boundary, and certification-only live gate explicit while allowing deterministic dry-run planning
GCP VM certification smoke proves a reviewed target is running without an external NAT IP, uses the Workcell preview broker plan, and accepts audited IAP SSH access
Host-side session inventory and detached control-plane commands cover durable records plus public CLI attach/send/stop/delete lifecycle handling for running and terminal workloads
Claude guard-bash.sh blocks all commands in the fixture file
